commentaries.author: Apringius of Beja
commentaries.content: a strong angel. this should be interpreted as the chorus of holy fathers who, having lived in divine friendship, and seeing with the eyes of faith that the arrangement of the present times and the order of all things were entirely sealed by the providence of God, understand their Operator to be the Lord of majesty.

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: strong angel. He indicates the promulgation of the Law.

commentaries.author: Caesarius of Arles
commentaries.content: who is worthy.... It is because Christ opened the book when he undertook the work of the Father's will, was conceived, and was born. The He broke its seals when He was slain for the human race.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: This was an angel of any kind.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: nor to look upon it. because they were not able to understand it

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Lyra
commentaries.content: who is worthy to open the book. That is, to reveal the mystery of God. and to loose the seals: That is, to show how each seal proceeds from the divine will.

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Lyra
commentaries.content: a strong angel. This seems to have been Gabrial who is interpreted as God’s might.

commentaries.author: Oecumenius
commentaries.content: who is worthy.... No one divine angel! One would say to him 'only the incarnate God.'

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: Now to open the book is to overcome death for man.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
